task_description:
# Reflex Blog with Dynamic Routing

## Background
Reflex is a full-stack Python framework that compiles Python UI definitions to a Next.js frontend and a FastAPI backend. In this task you will build a minimal blog with dynamic routing so each post is reachable at its own URL based on a slug segment.

## Requirements
- Build a Reflex application that:
  - Renders a home page at `/` listing exactly 3 hardcoded blog posts. Each post is an object with the fields `id`, `slug`, `title`, and `content`.
  - Renders a post detail page at the dynamic route `/posts/[slug]` showing a single post's title and content based on the `slug` URL segment.
  - Provides navigation from the index to each post detail page.
  - Shows a "Back to Posts" link on every detail page that navigates back to `/`.

## Implementation Hints
- Use the Reflex blank template and the `uv` package manager so that the dev environment is reproducible.
- Register the detail page with the route pattern `/posts/[slug]`, using either the `@rx.page(route=...)` decorator or `app.add_page(..., route=...)`.
- Read the dynamic slug from `self.router.page.params['slug']` (or the equivalent latest router API such as `self.router.url.path`) inside a state class, typically through a computed var.
- The list of posts can live as a class-level constant or as a base var. Keep the slugs unique across posts so each detail page is reachable.
- When the detail page receives an unknown slug, show a friendly fallback message instead of crashing.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Project path: /home/user/myproject
- Start command: `uv run reflex run`
- Ports: frontend on 3000, backend on 8000
- Routes:
  - `GET /` returns an HTML page that lists the title of each of the 3 posts and contains a link to that post's detail page under `/posts/<slug>`.
  - `GET /posts/<slug>` returns an HTML page that renders the title and content of the corresponding post and contains a link back to `/`.
- The Reflex source code must contain a page registration whose route is exactly `/posts/[slug]`, expressed using either `@rx.page(route="/posts/[slug]")` or `app.add_page(..., route="/posts/[slug]")`.
- The Reflex source code must access the dynamic slug through the Reflex router API (e.g., `self.router.page.params` or `self.router.url`) from inside a Reflex State subclass.
- The hardcoded posts collection must contain exactly 3 entries and each entry must have a unique non-empty `slug` value.
- Running `uv run reflex export --frontend-only --no-zip` inside the project directory must succeed and produce a Next.js build output that contains a generated page file for the dynamic route `posts/[slug]` (for example a path matching `.web/pages/posts/[slug].js`, `.web/.next/server/pages/posts/[slug].html`, or an equivalent file under `_export/`). The literal title of at least one of the three posts must appear somewhere in the generated build output.
- Kill all background servers started during the task (frontend, backend, dev server) before finishing.
task_integrations:
- Reflex (full-stack Python framework)
- uv (Astral package manager used to manage the Python environment)
truth:
# Verification Plan

## Setup
1. cd /home/user/myproject
2. Ensure no leftover Reflex processes from previous runs are listening on ports 3000 or 8000; kill any that exist.
3. If the directories `/home/user/myproject/.web` or `/home/user/myproject/_export` exist, leave them in place (they will be regenerated during verification).

## Verification Steps
1. **Static source checks (no execution):**
   - Search every `*.py` file under `/home/user/myproject` (excluding `.venv` and `.web`) for a route declaration matching `/posts/[slug]` registered via either `@rx.page(route="/posts/[slug]")` or `app.add_page(..., route="/posts/[slug]")`.
   - Search every `*.py` file under `/home/user/myproject` (excluding `.venv` and `.web`) for an access to the Reflex router API such as `router.page.params` or `router.url` from within code that defines or references a class inheriting from `rx.State`.
   - Search for a hardcoded posts collection. The source code must contain at least 3 distinct slug string literals. The expected default slugs are `hello-world`, `reflex-rocks`, and `third-post`, but any 3 unique non-empty slugs are acceptable as long as the same 3 slugs are also reflected in the rendered HTML.
   - Save the 3 detected slugs and the corresponding post titles for use in later steps.

2. **Frontend-only export build:**
   - Run `uv run reflex export --frontend-only --no-zip` inside `/home/user/myproject`. The command must exit with status 0 within 5 minutes.
   - After the export completes, at least one of the following must exist (the verifier should check all candidates):
     - `/home/user/myproject/.web/pages/posts/[slug].js`
     - `/home/user/myproject/.web/.next/server/pages/posts/[slug].html`
     - `/home/user/myproject/.web/.next/server/pages/posts/[slug].js`
     - A file whose path matches the glob `/home/user/myproject/_export/**/posts/[slug]*` (any extension).
   - Recursively search the generated build directory (`/home/user/myproject/.web` and `/home/user/myproject/_export` if present) for the literal title text of any one of the three detected posts. The title must appear at least once in the build output.

3. **Runtime check (best-effort, only if the source checks all pass):**
   - Start the application with `uv run reflex run` as a background process and wait up to 120 seconds for the frontend to become reachable on `http://localhost:3000/`.
   - Fetch `http://localhost:3000/` with curl. The HTML must contain the title of each of the 3 posts.
   - For at least one of the detected slugs `<slug>`, fetch `http://localhost:3000/posts/<slug>`. The HTML must contain the matching title and the matching content for that slug, plus a link whose `href` is `/` (the "Back to Posts" link).
   - The runtime check is best-effort: if the Reflex dev server cannot bind to the ports (for example, in a constrained CI sandbox), the verifier may skip this step. The export-based checks in step 2 are the authoritative verification.

4. **Cleanup:**
   - Kill every background Reflex server (`reflex run`, `next`, `uvicorn`, etc.) started during verification. Confirm that nothing is listening on ports 3000 or 8000 at the end of the test.
